summary.what: Extend pending-detection: drop empty-file merges from display, .timbersignore author globs, TIMBERS_DEBUG=1 trace, and timbers ack command
summary.why: osprey-strike friction report — Laura saw merge SHAs in pending with no obvious next action, and the impending q-redshifted autofix pipeline needed a bot-author skip path; ack gives an honest skip-with-reason instead of fabrication or --no-verify
summary.how: ParentCount on git.Commit via %P enables dropEmptyMerges in display path. .timbersignore extended with 'author:<glob>' lines via classifyTimbersIgnoreLine; same parser yields both rule shapes. Debug trace in skipcount.go gated on TIMBERS_DEBUG envtruthy. Ack stored under .timbers/YYYY/MM/DD/ack_*.json with kind='ack' under timbers.devlog/v1; AckedSet threaded through filterCommits parallel to docSet (single scan per pending check)

notes: Originally implemented skip-authors as a dedicated .timbers/skip-authors file. User pushed back on file sprawl — folded it into .timbersignore via 'author:' line prefix, which is more idiomatic (mirrors .gitignore family) and gives single source of truth for repo skip config. Edge case worth documenting: the 'author:' prefix collides with literal paths starting with 'author:' (extremely rare since ':' is forbidden in Windows filenames). Reviewer caught the AckedSet double-scan and the dead pushedMsg parameter on round 1; round 2 caught a checklist-ordering test gap and an author/ trace error. Five beads' worth of work in one commit because storage.go and skipcount.go are touched by all of them — interactive staging would have been more friction than value.
